finding: Pre-push and pre-commit hook failures surface as one large raw JSON blob (>100 KB observed) on host git stderr; the blocking finding and `requiredCommand` are buried, so both human and AI operators must grep persisted output to find the actual remediation.
expectedBehavior: Hook failures should print a compact human summary first (top blocking finding + exact requiredCommand), then write the full JSON to a report file path referenced in the summary.
evidenceOrRepro: 2026-07-11 `git push origin main` block for `ATM_TASK_AUDIT_BULK_CLOSE_WITHOUT_MANIFEST` produced ~118 KB of JSON through stderr across two attempts before the finding was located.
followUp: Add a summary-first formatter to hook stderr with `--json-report <path>` style overflow.
